data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Starts Proceed Investment Corporation ( (JP:8979) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Starts Proceed Investment Corporation has revised its management status and distribution forecast for the fiscal period ending October 2026, reflecting improved expectations following recent asset acquisitions. Operating revenue is now projected at ¥3,683 million and net income at ¥1,160 million, representing modest increases of around 1–2% from prior guidance.

The REIT now forecasts a distribution per unit of ¥4,650, up ¥50 from the previous estimate, while the portion classified as distribution in excess of earnings remains unchanged. The revision is driven mainly by higher anticipated revenue from newly acquired specified assets, signaling incremental earnings growth for investors and a slightly stronger outlook for the REIT’s near‑term performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:8979) stock is a Hold with a Yen207957.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Starts Proceed Investment Corporation stock, see the JP:8979 Stock Forecast page.

More about Starts Proceed Investment Corporation

Starts Proceed Investment Corporation is a Japanese real estate investment trust focused on acquiring and managing domestic real estate assets. Listed under code 8979 and managed by Starts Asset Management Co., Ltd., it targets stable operating revenue and distributions through active portfolio management in Japan’s property market.

The latest announcement is out from Patagonia Lithium Limited ( (AU:PL3) ).

Patagonia Lithium Ltd has lodged its Appendix 4G and 2025 Corporate Governance Statement with the ASX, confirming its governance disclosures for the year ended 31 December 2025 are available on its website and have been approved by the board. The filing underscores the company’s adherence to ASX corporate governance requirements, a key factor for institutional and retail investors assessing transparency and board oversight.

The company also flagged that its 2026 annual general meeting will be held on 28 May 2026, with the re-election of directors on the agenda and a 9 April 2026 deadline for board nomination submissions. The timetable formalises the upcoming governance and board renewal process, giving shareholders and potential director candidates a defined window to influence the company’s leadership and strategic direction.

Patagonia Lithium Ltd is an Australian-listed company focused on lithium exploration and development, positioning itself within the battery metals sector that underpins the energy transition. The company targets lithium assets in prospective regions, aiming to supply future demand from electric vehicle and energy storage markets.

data.title: Standard Chartered Sets Date for 2026 AGM and Puts 2025 Final Dividend to Shareholder Vote
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Standard Chartered ( (GB:STAN) ) has provided an announcement.

Standard Chartered has published its 2026 Notice of Annual General Meeting and made it available to shareholders and regulators, alongside previously released 2025 Annual Report and Pillar 3 disclosures on its investor website. The AGM will be held as a physical-only meeting at London’s Honourable Artillery Company on 7 May 2026, where shareholders will be asked to approve a recommended final dividend of 49 US cents per ordinary share for 2025 and will have the opportunity to question the board, underscoring the bank’s emphasis on capital returns and shareholder engagement.

The bank has outlined practical details for attending the meeting and channels for submitting questions in advance, while reserving the right to alter the AGM format and communicate any changes via its website and regulatory news services. The announcement also confirms the current composition of the board, providing governance transparency that may reassure investors focused on oversight, accountability and the continuity of the group’s leadership as it enters the next financial year.

More about Standard Chartered

Standard Chartered PLC is a UK-headquartered international banking group incorporated in England and Wales, with its shares listed in Hong Kong under stock code 02888. The bank provides a broad range of financial services across global markets, with a significant footprint in Asia, the Middle East and Africa, serving retail, corporate and institutional clients.

See more insights into STAN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Sequoia Financial Group Ltd. ( (AU:SEQ) ) has provided an update.

Sequoia Financial Group Ltd has disclosed a change in the interests of director Garry Peter Crole, who holds both direct and indirect stakes in the company’s fully paid ordinary shares. The filing notes that his interests include holdings through his own account, a family superannuation fund, and shares held by his spouse.

On 26 March 2026, Crole acquired 44,569 Sequoia shares via an on-market trade for a consideration of $10,250, with no shares disposed. Following this transaction, his direct holding remained at 11,858,470 shares, while his indirect holdings were consolidated and increased through the family super fund, signalling a modest uplift in his overall exposure to the company.

StepChange Holdings Ltd. ( (AU:STH) ) has shared an announcement.

StepChange Holdings director Geoff Lewis has increased his direct holding in the company through on-market purchases made on 24 and 26 March 2026. He acquired 165,000 ordinary fully paid shares for $18,775, lifting his freely tradeable stake while his larger escrowed holding of 23,712,000 shares remains unchanged.

Following the transactions, Lewis now holds 2,665,000 non-escrowed ordinary shares in addition to his escrowed block, reinforcing his financial exposure to the company’s equity. The trades were conducted outside a closed period and did not involve any disposals, signaling continued confidence from a key insider without altering existing lock-up arrangements.

data.title: NZX to launch S&P/NZX 20 index futures in April to deepen local equity market
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from NZX ( (NZSTF) ).

NZX will launch S&P/NZX 20 Index Futures on 28 April 2026, giving investors a local, low-cost tool to hedge or gain exposure to the New Zealand equity market and aligning the country with other developed markets that have liquid index futures. Backed by a Cornerstone Group of major institutional investors and a designated market maker in BNP Paribas, the contracts are designed to boost liquidity in NZX-listed stocks, support KiwiSaver and institutional portfolio management, and lay the groundwork for a broader domestic equity derivatives platform.

The exchange has upgraded its trading, clearing and connectivity infrastructure and secured connectivity via Trading Technologies, enabling both global and domestic participants to access the new futures. By setting the contract size at $1 per index point and planning for retail broker and platform integration over time, NZX aims to expand participation across the market, deepen on-market activity and restore a local futures capability that had previously migrated offshore.

More about NZX

NZX is New Zealand’s primary securities exchange, operating the country’s equity, debt, funds, derivatives and energy markets while providing trading, clearing, settlement, depository and data services. The group also owns ETF issuer Smart, KiwiSaver provider SuperLife, and fund manager QuayStreet Asset Management, and delivers platform technology through NZX Wealth Technologies for local investment advisers and providers.

See more insights into NZSTF stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Keybridge Capital Limited ( (AU:KBC) ) has provided an announcement.

Keybridge Capital Limited has strengthened its board with the appointment of Andrew Moffat as a non-executive director, marking the return of a former chairman who previously served on the board between 2014 and 2016. Moffat brings more than 25 years of corporate and investment banking experience, including senior roles in equity capital markets and advisory, and currently holds multiple non-executive directorships across listed and unlisted entities.

The company’s directors say Moffat’s expertise will be invaluable as Keybridge pursues a rebuilding phase, signaling a renewed focus on governance and strategic repositioning for its diversified investment portfolio. His appointment may bolster investor confidence by reinforcing board depth and oversight at a time when the group is seeking to stabilise and enhance the performance of its varied holdings.

data.title: ISB Corporation Issues Restricted Stock to Align Management Pay With Shareholder Value
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ISB CORP (Japan) ( (JP:9702) ) just unveiled an update.

ISB Corporation has approved the issuance of 15,356 new shares of common stock as restricted stock remuneration to six directors and nine executive officers, with a total value of ¥31,571,936 and a payment date set for April 27, 2026. The shares will be allocated through monetary compensation claims converted in kind, at an issue price of ¥2,056 per share, matching the prior day’s Tokyo Stock Exchange closing price to ensure alignment with market value and avoid preferential treatment.

The move is part of a restricted share-based remuneration plan first introduced in 2020 to incentivize eligible directors and executive officers to enhance long-term corporate value and strengthen alignment with shareholders. By tying a portion of management compensation to company stock under restricted conditions, ISB aims to reinforce value sharing with investors and support sustained performance and governance objectives without materially advantaging insiders over the broader market.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:9702) stock is a Hold with a Yen2232.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ISB CORP (Japan) stock, see the JP:9702 Stock Forecast page.

More about ISB CORP (Japan)

ISB Corporation is a Japan-based company listed under securities code 9702, operating in the information services and systems sector. The company focuses on providing technology-driven solutions and corporate services, with a governance structure that includes a board of directors, executive officers, and audit and supervisory committee members as part of its management and oversight framework.
